page: Microsoft Azure E96ias_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EIASv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a base speed of 3.35 GHz and includes an L1d cache of 32 KB, L2 cache of 0.5 MB, and L3 cache of 16 MB. The instance is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.0 GB per core. It also includes 1376 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. The instance exhibits strong memory performance, with cached read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 3921.34 GB/sec and a strong PassMark memory score of 2895.04. Multi-threaded operations perform well, as evidenced by a strong bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 415.45 GB/sec and a strong PassMark CPU score of 68844.02. Cryptographic capabilities are particularly notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test placing in the top 10% at 96943.13. However, single-core performance is a relative bottleneck; the stress-ng single-core CPU test scores in the bottom 10% at 770.89, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the E96ias_v4 offers high memory capacity and dedicated compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-bound enterprise workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The primary tradeoff lies in its single-core processing limits, meaning it is less suited for highly sequential, single-threaded tasks. However, its high core count and strong memory bandwidth make it highly effective for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, Redis deployments achieving 72.45 million operations per second, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ias_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the EIASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs, 672.0 GB of RAM, and 1376 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong multi-threaded performance, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark encryption score and strong cached memory bandwidth of 3921.34 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng. This resource profile makes the E96ias_v4 highly suitable for memory-intensive, multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and software compilation, while offering a balanced, high-density hardware configuration without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96ias_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1376 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-threaded and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96ias_v4 is an isolated instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1376 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 672 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory and 1376 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities
Strong cached memory bandwidth of 3921.34 GB/sec and high Redis throughput
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and enterprise workloads
